steps.title: Use the sign pattern
steps.content: The function $f(x)=(x-a)(x-b)$ opens upward, so it is positive outside its two roots and negative between them.

steps.title: Place the two roots
steps.content: The values are positive at $12$, negative at $15$, and positive at $18$. Therefore, one integer root must be between $12$ and $15$, and the other must be between $15$ and $18$.

steps.title: Test the possible sums
steps.content:
The possible integer roots are $13$ or $14$ for the first interval and $16$ or $17$ for the second interval:
$$13+16=29,\quad 13+17=30,\quad 14+16=30,\quad 14+17=31$$
The answer is D.
